name: Cold Chicken, Celery and Walnut Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove all skin and fat from the chicken legs. Place them in a saucepan with one onion, carrot, peppercorns, and a pinch of sea salt. Cover with water and bring to a boil. Simmer for 40-45 minutes.
2. Meanwhile, chop the remaining onion and celery. Put them in a heavy, covered pan with olive oil and soften.
3. When the vegetables are soft and chicken is cooked through, strain most of the stock from around the chicken over the vegetables. Continue simmering for another 15-20 minutes.
4. Strip the chicken meat from the bones and cover it with one spoonful of stock to keep it moist. Discard the bones, carrot, and onion.
5. In a mortar, crush garlic with sea salt. Pound walnuts into this mixture until quite fine. Add chicken meat (soft), yoghurt, and pepper. Mix well.
6. Blend the celery soup briefly, then stir in the chicken and nut mixture. Chill before serving.
() You can first crush walnuts in an electric blender; avoid adding garlic as it may affect taste.
() This soup is best enjoyed cold, but also delicious warm.
